shortDescription.text: LDAP filter injection via unsanitized user input

fullDescription.text: The 'username' parameter from user-supplied JSON is interpolated directly into an LDAP filter string using an f-string without any escaping or validation. An attacker can inject LDAP filter metacharacters (e.g., *, ), |, &) to manipulate the query logic, potentially bypassing authentication, enumerating users, or extracting directory data.

shortDescription.text: Arbitrary code execution via eval() on user input

fullDescription.text: User-supplied 'code' parameter is passed directly to Python's eval() function, allowing arbitrary code execution on the server. This is the most critical class of vulnerability as it grants full server compromise.

shortDescription.text: OS command injection via subprocess.run with shell=True

fullDescription.text: User-supplied 'command' parameter is passed directly to subprocess.run() with shell=True, allowing arbitrary OS command execution. An attacker can execute any system command with the privileges of the web server process.

shortDescription.text: SSRF via unvalidated URL in requests.get()

fullDescription.text: User-supplied 'url' parameter is passed directly to requests.get() without any URL validation or allowlist. An attacker can probe internal network services (e.g., cloud metadata endpoints at 169.254.169.254, internal APIs), scan ports, and exfiltrate data from otherwise unreachable hosts.

shortDescription.text: Jinja2 SSTI via unsandboxed Template rendering

fullDescription.text: User-supplied 'template' string is passed to Jinja2's Template() constructor without sandboxing. An attacker can use Jinja2's introspection capabilities (e.g., __class__.__mro__) to escape the template context and achieve arbitrary code execution.

shortDescription.text: Arbitrary code execution via pickle.loads() on user input

fullDescription.text: User-supplied base64-encoded data is decoded and passed to pickle.loads(). Python's pickle module can execute arbitrary code during deserialization via __reduce__, granting full server compromise.

shortDescription.text: Path traversal in file upload via unsanitized filename

fullDescription.text: The uploaded file's original filename is used directly in os.path.join() without sanitization. An attacker can use '../' sequences in the filename to write files to arbitrary locations on the filesystem.

shortDescription.text: Hardcoded JWT secret and API keys in source code

fullDescription.text: JWT signing secret and API keys are hardcoded in source code. An attacker with access to the source (or who can guess the weak secret) can forge JWT tokens and impersonate any user including administrators.

shortDescription.text: Password logged in plaintext via audit logger

fullDescription.text: The login endpoint logs the user's plaintext password in the audit log. This exposes credentials to anyone with log access and violates the principle of least privilege for sensitive data.

message.text: LDAP filter injection: the 'username' parameter from request JSON is interpolated directly into the LDAP filter string '(&(objectClass=user)(cn={username}))' via f-string without escaping. An attacker can inject metacharacters like *)(&(objectClass=*) to manipulate filter logic, bypass authentication, or enumerate directory entries.

Arbitrary code execution: user-supplied 'code' parameter is passed directly to eval(). An attacker can execute arbitrary Python expressions including __import__('os').system('...') to gain full control of the server.

OS command injection: user-supplied 'command' parameter is executed directly via subprocess.run() with shell=True. An attacker can chain arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters (;, |, &&) for full system compromise.

Server-Side Request Forgery: user-supplied 'url' is passed to requests.get() without validation. An attacker can access internal services (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ for cloud credentials), scan internal networks, and exfiltrate data via DNS or HTTP.

Server-Side Template Injection: user-supplied 'template' string is rendered by Jinja2 without sandboxing. An attacker can escape the template context via {{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }} to achieve arbitrary code execution.

Unsafe deserialization: user-supplied base64 data is decoded and passed to pickle.loads(). An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload with __reduce__ to execute arbitrary code on deserialization.

Path traversal: the uploaded file's original filename is used in os.path.join() without sanitization. An attacker can use filenames like '../../etc/cron.d/backdoor' to write files outside the upload directory.
Hardcoded JWT secret 'super_secret_key_123' and API keys in source code. An attacker can forge valid JWT tokens to impersonate any user, including administrators.
Plaintext password logged in audit trail: f"User {username} logged in with password {password}". Anyone with log access can harvest credentials.
